mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when either series is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production above its 12-month moving average confirms robust manufacturing capacity utilization, supporting earnings growth and generating positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO above trend coincides with negative 20d returns in >58% of cases during tightening periods, or the signal IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing rapid portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in >65% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 20d returns in >55% of quarters, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% signals abrupt supply chain contraction and weakening economic activity, prompting immediate equity sell-offs and negative returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative production shocks are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the trigger fails to separate winning from losing periods statistically.
